title: The finite DC mode of a constant signal is cardinality times the constant

source.citation: Lloyd N. Trefethen, Spectral Methods in MATLAB, SIAM, 2000, Ch. 3, pp. 17-28.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For any finite index set, the DC sum of a constant real-valued signal equals the number of sampled entries times the constant.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
informal.expected_conclusion: The finite DC mode is the cardinality of s times c.

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteDC_const
declarations.statement: theorem finiteDC_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (c : Real) : finiteDC s (fun _ => c) = (s.card : Real) * c

tasks.input: Translate the finite DC mode of a constant signal into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteDC_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (c : Real) : finiteDC s (fun _ => c) = (s.card : Real) * c

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding finiteDC, summing a constant over a finite set, and normalizing scalar multiplication.
Find the finite-sum lemma for summing a constant over a finite set.
